cases.input: Create accessibility tests for /checkout covering the menu, payment modal, form validation errors, and success toast with Playwright and axe.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill produces stateful automated test guidance that opens each dynamic state before scanning, records command/tool/version, rule IDs, selectors, impacts, WCAG tags, artifacts, and refuses to treat a single initial page scan as full proof.

cases.input: Use jest-axe to prove this React button component is accessible, including contrast and keyboard behavior.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill uses jest-axe for component semantics while marking browser-rendered contrast and keyboard behavior as needing browser/manual evidence instead of overclaiming.

cases.input: Build a keyboard-only test script for the account settings modal and navigation drawer.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill outputs a matrix with flow, step, keys, expected focus/action, observed result placeholder, pass/fail/not-verified status, and evidence fields covering Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, Escape, arrows where relevant, focus trap, and focus restoration.

cases.input: Create screen reader smoke tests for the onboarding form using VoiceOver/Safari and NVDA/Firefox.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill provides smoke scripts with OS/browser/assistive-technology pairing, expected announcements, observed announcements, status, and coverage for landmarks, headings, names/roles/values, form errors, live regions, dialogs, and reading order.

cases.input: Validate contrast for primary, disabled, hover, focus, placeholder, error, and icon-only states in our design tokens.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill produces a contrast matrix with selector/token, foreground/background, state, observed ratio, threshold, status, and explicitly distinguishes text, large text, and non-text UI checks.

cases.input: Add reduced-motion accessibility regression coverage for the dashboard animation and auto-scrolling notification tray.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill specifies reduced-motion test setup, expected behavior under reduced motion, observed status, artifact path, and marks non-essential motion as a user-impact risk when it remains enabled.

cases.input: I ran axe on document.body before opening anything. Write the accessibility pass report.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill refuses a blanket pass, marks menus/dialogs/errors/toasts/tooltips as not verified, requests target scope, and explains that unopened dynamic states invalidate the report.

cases.input: Exclude .legacy-widget from all axe checks forever because it is noisy.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill rejects broad permanent suppression and requires issue ID, owner, expiry, selector, rule ID, reason, and re-enable criteria.

cases.input: Run accessibility testing and fix everything you find.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill separates testing from remediation, asks or confirms whether code changes are authorized, and otherwise produces findings, recommended fixes, and retest criteria without silent edits.

cases.input: Make this login screen look cleaner and more modern.
cases.expected_behavior: Skill should not take over as primary workflow; it may suggest accessibility testing as a follow-up but routes primary work to design or UI implementation skills.

cases.input: What is the weather forecast for tomorrow?
cases.expected_behavior: Skill should not activate because the request is unrelated to accessibility testing.
